purpose: Record user-owned pass/fail/why decisions for candidate and case regression.
rules: Only user verdicts can confirm cases for formal regression.
Agent notes may be referenced but cannot supply final pass/fail.
Do not quote long source text in ledger entries.

why: User review confirms the old dialogue-length problem remains: too many short answers and too few mid-length utterances. User also identifies a separate source-shape problem: dialogue runs chain too long; source rhythm is answer, then thought/object/body paragraph, and if one question-answer pair is counted as one unit, the source rarely exceeds 5 and is usually 1-2.
follow_up: Add dialogue-run constraints to next candidate spec and prompt.
Measure or manually audit contiguous question-answer run length separately from dialogue length bins.
Repair by inserting Adachi thought/object/body paragraphs between one or two Q-A pairs, not by merely padding short lines.

why: User identified that the current agent/prompt language is too attached to originality. Forcing originality can suppress useful imitation of style mechanisms, rhythm, translation texture, dialogue timing, and interior-monologue shape.
follow_up: Remove dominant originality and anti-imitation phrasing from active prompt policy.
Keep copying boundaries narrow: no source text copying, no long excerpts, no exact phrasing or scene tracing.
Make style-mechanism emulation an explicit generation target.
